title: gnaphalium sordescens
intro: Gnaphalium sordescens, also known as bitter cudweed, is a perennial plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin conditions. The plant's medicinal properties are believed to help alleviate symptoms of eczema, acne, and other skin irritations, while its health benefits include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Gnaphalium sordescens is often prepared into teas, tinctures, and infusions for internal use, as well as topical creams and ointments for external applications.
